name: Guidelines for Migrating to openEuler | openEuler
description: Effortlessly migrate to openEuler with our comprehensive solutions, enhancing system efficiency, security, and performance

name: How to Migrate to openEuler
description: A comprehensive six-step guide for migrating your operating system to openEuler, including team setup, needs analysis, planning, software adaptation, OS migration, and testing.

step.name: Create a Migration Support Team
step.text: Set up a migration support team comprising members from your company's service, IT, and maintenance departments, as well as from the OSV and ISV. The team will provide technical support and organizational assurance during the operation.

step.name: Analyze Migration Needs
step.text: Determine the project overview by collecting the names and requirements of services to be migrated, and the software stacks, deployment modes, and server hardware information. Filter information by service provider, OS, and hardware environment, with specified service priority. Assess compatibility in an assessment report.

step.name: Design a Migration Plan
step.text: Based on the migration analysis, design a comprehensive migration plan that addresses all identified compatibility and operational requirements.

step.name: Adapt Software
step.text: Adapt the required software components before migration using x2openEuler to perform compatibility tests and batch analysis of nodes to be upgraded.

step.name: Migrate the OS
step.text: Use x2openEuler to perform in-place upgrade of OSs in batches. Batch add nodes to be upgraded, then batch upgrade the adapted nodes to achieve an end-to-end seamless migration to openEuler.

step.name: Perform Testing and Service Rollout
step.text: Conduct thorough testing and perform service rollout to validate the migration was successful and ensure system stability and performance.
